channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cape Town tenders to buy 500MW from private power traders to lower electricity costs
item.description: The City of Cape Town (CoCT) has announced a major initiative to reduce its dependence on Eskom by procuring 500 megawatts of electricity from licensed private energy traders. The move aims to cut ele...

item.title: NTC prepares measures to ensure steady communication services during Holy Week 2026
item.description: The National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) has rolled out nationwide efforts to guarantee stable and continuous communication throughout Holy Week 2026. In a public statement issued on March 30,...

item.title: White House suggests Gulf nations could contribute financially to military campaign
item.description: According to reports from Washington, the White House is considering a plan under which Gulf states may be asked to help finance a potential military campaign led by the United States. Senior official...

item.title: Names of Victims Killed, Kidnapped, and Injured in Southern Kaduna Wedding Attack Released
item.description: For Sunday, di people wey dey Kukyer and Kahir villages, Kagarko LGA, Kaduna State, suffer heavy wahala as bandits attack wedding ceremony. Di attack lead to 13 people wey dem kill, 28 people wey dem ...

item.title: How to Bake Delicious Homemade Hot Cross Buns with Beatrix Bakes' Recipe
item.description: In this Easter-themed feature for The Canberra Times, journalist Karen Hardy invites readers to skip mass-produced supermarket hot cross buns and instead bake their own using a detailed recipe from Na...

item.title: Johannesburg petrol stations limit fuel sales amid supply delays linked to Middle East conflict
item.description: Several petrol stations across Johannesburg have begun rationing fuel, restricting motorists to 30 litres per vehicle as fuel supply delays linked to the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and I...

item.title: Public Consultation in Manitoba on Social Media Restrictions for Minors
item.description: Residents of Manitoba are sharing their perspectives on a proposed regulation that would prevent children under 16 from using social media platforms. The discussion comes amid growing concerns over th...

item.title: Nairobi Woman Shares Screenshots of Boda Boda Rider Commenting on Her Outfit
item.description: A Nairobi lady named Jos recently shared screenshots on her TikTok showing texts she received from a boda boda rider commenting on her dressing style. The rider began with a polite greeting and explai...

item.title: Master Hanz Cua's Feng Shui Guidance for March 31, 2026
item.description: Master Hanz Cua, a recognized feng shui expert in the Philippines, shares his daily insights and guidance for March 31, 2026. The article provides general advice on how Filipinos can harmonize their s...

item.title: Bafana Bafana coach finalises most of World Cup squad
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has confirmed that he has about 75% of his World Cup squad already decided. Speaking at a press conference in Cape Town, Broos emphasised that even a poor performance in...

item.title: Iloilo City and San Fernando Recognized by the UN for Leading Zero-Waste Initiatives
item.description: The United Nations has named Iloilo City and San Fernando, Pampanga as part of its '20 Cities Towards Zero Waste' initiative, honoring local governments that show innovation and commitment to sustaina...

item.title: David Attenborough explores British gardens and their wildlife in Secret Garden
item.description: David Attenborough, approaching his 100th birthday, is set to present a new BBC series titled Secret Garden, which focuses on the remarkable biodiversity of British gardens. Unlike his previous progra...

item.title: Canadian startup SBQuantum tests diamond quantum sensors in orbit to map Earth's magnetic field
item.description: SBQuantum, a technology startup from Sherbrooke, Quebec, has achieved a significant milestone by successfully sending its diamond-based quantum magnetometers into space aboard a SpaceX rocket launched...

item.title: How fake beauty products are putting Kenyans at risk
item.description: Kenyans are being warned about the growing dangers of fake beauty products circulating in the market. Experts, including F. Mukembu, caution that many women fall victim to enticing advertisements on T...

item.title: Former FCT Minister Jumoke Akinjide Resigns From PDP After Two Decades
item.description: Former Minister of State for the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Jumoke Akinjide, don resign from di Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) afta she don waka with dem for more than twenty-four years. For her...

item.title: Police shooting suspect Desmond Freeman shot dead after seven-month manhunt in Victoria
item.description: After a seven-month search, alleged police killer Desmond ‘Dezi’ Freeman has been shot dead by heavily armed officers in Victoria’s north-east. Freeman was accused of murdering two police officers and...

item.title: Vedanta seeks Supreme Court intervention against Adani’s approved acquisition of Jaypee assets
item.description: Mining conglomerate Vedanta has approached the Supreme Court of India seeking a stay on the acquisition of the insolvent Jaypee Group’s assets by Gautam Adani’s Adani Group. The legal action follows t...

item.title: Grasslands National Park restricts access to protect endangered bird habitat
item.description: Grasslands National Park in Saskatchewan has implemented temporary access restrictions to a section of its protected area in order to safeguard an endangered bird species. Park officials emphasize tha...

item.title: Ramaphosa Sets Up Task Team to Cushion South Africans From April Fuel and Food Price Hikes
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced the formation of a ministerial task team to find ways of protecting South Africans from the steep fuel price increase expected in April. The announcement came d...

item.title: Economic Pressures Mount as Iran Conflict Continues Without Diplomatic Solutions
item.description: On the 31st day of the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, the economic consequences of the war are becoming more severe. Military operations remain intense across multiple fronts, including Iran, Israel, ...
